content.title: Exponential Functions
content.content:
- An **exponential function** has form $f(x) = b^x$ where $b > 0$, $b \neq 1$

- Domain: all real numbers; Range: $(0, \infty)$

- $y$-intercept: $(0, 1)$; Horizontal asymptote: $y = 0$

- If $b > 1$: growth; If $0 < b < 1$: decay

- Exponential functions are one-to-one: if $b^m = b^n$, then $m = n$

content.title: The Natural Base $e$
content.content:
- $e = \lim_{n \to \infty}\left(1 + \frac{1}{n}\right)^n \approx 2.71828$

- $e^x$ is called the natural exponential function

- The derivative of $e^x$ equals $e^x$ (its rate of change equals its value)

- $e$ arises naturally in continuous growth/decay processes

content.title: Compound Interest Formulas
content.content:
- **Compound interest:** $A = P\left(1 + \frac{r}{n}\right)^{nt}$

- **Continuous compounding:** $A = Pe^{rt}$

- **Rule of 72:** Doubling time $\approx 72/r\%$

- **Present value:** $P = \frac{A}{(1 + r/n)^{nt}}$ or $P = Ae^{-rt}$

- **APY:** $\left(1 + \frac{r}{n}\right)^n - 1$

content.title: Exponential Growth and Decay
content.content:
- General model: $N(t) = N_0 e^{kt}$

- $k > 0$: growth; $k < 0$: decay

- **Doubling time:** $t = \frac{\ln 2}{k}$

- **Half-life:** $t_{1/2} = \frac{\ln 2}{|k|}$

- **Newton's Law of Cooling:** $T(t) = T_s + (T_0 - T_s)e^{-kt}$
